root_cause: The web service was running RELEASE=v2, a bad deploy that caused readiness failures and returned HTTP 500s from the checkout /orders path. The database and neighboring payments service were healthy; the failure was isolated to the web release configuration.
actions_taken: Inspected running Docker containers, web state, environment, logs, compose configuration, and the checkout endpoint. Confirmed web was on RELEASE=v2 and /orders returned 500. Updated only the web service RELEASE value in docker-compose.yaml from v2 to v1, recreated only the web container with docker compose up -d web, then verified the container was running RELEASE=v1 and /orders returned HTTP 200 with {"ok":true,"release":"v1"}.
postmortem: A regressed application release reached production without being blocked before checkout traffic was affected. The least-risk remediation was a targeted rollback of web to the last known good release, avoiding changes to database state or unrelated services. Follow-up should add or strengthen pre-deploy and post-deploy checks for /orders, ensure readiness reflects critical checkout behavior, and keep rollback runbooks and release metadata explicit so future incidents can be mitigated quickly and safely.
